title: The Fox and the Grapes
synopsis: A hungry fox spots a beautiful bunch of grapes hanging high above him. A story about how people excuse what they cannot reach.
sentences: One afternoon, a hungry Fox walked through a vineyard.
He soon saw a beautiful bunch of ripe grapes hanging from a high vine.
The grapes looked cool, sweet, and perfect for a hot day.
The Fox stepped back, ran forward, and jumped as high as he could.
But he missed the grapes by a small distance.
He tried again and again, leaping with all his strength.
Each time, the grapes stayed just out of reach.
At last, the Fox stopped and looked at them with an angry face.
"Those grapes are probably sour anyway," he said.
With that, he turned away and walked off proudly.
The Fox wanted to hide his disappointment from himself.
Sometimes people pretend they do not want what they cannot have.
